reasoning_model_1: The model failed significantly in Content Accuracy. Instead of reading the provided script ('最近天气变化大，我感觉有点着凉了...'), the model completely paraphrased the text into a different casual style ('最近天气真是说变就变，我这嗓子都快冒烟了...'). Regarding the required paralinguistic feature 'Non-verbal vocalization' (coughing), the model merely vocalized the onomatopoeia 'Ke Ke' (咳咳) at the beginning (00:00) rather than generating an actual non-verbal coughing sound. The tone remained lively and energetic, contradicting the context of being sick. Due to the complete inconsistency with the script content and the failure to produce non-verbal sounds, this is rated as a failure.
reasoning_model_2: The model followed the script perfectly. In terms of 'Non-verbal vocalization', it successfully generated realistic coughing sounds at the beginning (00:00-00:02) corresponding to the '咳咳……' in the prompt. The coughing sounded natural, deep, and visceral, not like spoken words. Furthermore, the model maintained a weak, breathy, and slightly hoarse timbre throughout the speech (e.g., at 00:04 '感觉有点着凉了'), which aligns perfectly with the persona of someone who is sick. The integration of the non-verbal feature with the speech was seamless and highly natural.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed on two major fronts: it hallucinated/rewrote the script content entirely, and it failed to generate the required non-verbal sound effect, instead speaking the words 'Ke Ke'. Model 2 produced accurate content, realistic non-verbal coughing sounds, and an appropriate 'sick' tone that enhanced the realism of the non-verbal feature.
